title: helix-cli: Staging Merge Conflict Resolution (BLD-448)
description: This tour walks through the conflict resolution run for helix-cli ticket BLD-448 (Library Comments and Iteration). A staging refresh introduced a reported merge conflict in src/tickets/index.ts between the ticket branch (library CLI integration, 4 commits) and the staging branch (concurrent ticket command updates, 1 commit). The agent verified that the conflict was already auto-resolved with zero conflict markers remaining, all 10 subcommand handlers preserved, and the TypeScript build passing cleanly. No source code changes were required — this tour documents the verification artifacts and decision rationale.

steps.description: The run was triggered by a staging refresh that listed src/tickets/index.ts as conflicted between 4 ticket branch commits (library CLI implementation + prior ticket lookup changes) and 1 staging commit (concurrent ticket command dispatcher updates). This diagnosis statement is the entry point — it documents the problem, root cause, and evidence gathered before any implementation decisions were made.
steps.title: Overview: What triggered this run

steps.description: The conflict arose because both branches independently modified the same ticket command dispatcher module (src/tickets/index.ts). The ticket branch added library CLI integration; the staging branch added update-description and other improvements. The diagnosis confirmed the conflict was pre-resolved: zero matches for '<<<<<<<', '=======', or '>>>>>>>' in the file or anywhere in src/. The 150-line file is a clean switch-based dispatcher with all 10 expected subcommand imports.
steps.title: Root Cause: Concurrent dispatcher edits

steps.description: The APL (Audit/Planning Log) for diagnosis captures the three key questions the agent asked itself: (1) Are there unresolved conflicts in tickets/index.ts? — No, grep returned zero hits. (2) Are there stray markers elsewhere in src/? — No, all 16 grep hits were in .helix/ artifact JSON files from prior runs, not source files. (3) Does the resolved file preserve both ticket and staging intents? — Yes, all 10 subcommand imports and switch cases are present. This structured Q&A pattern ensures no assumption goes unverified.
steps.title: Diagnosis APL: Three verification questions answered

steps.description: Because the conflict was pre-resolved, the implementation plan explicitly states zero source file modifications are required. The three steps are purely verificational: (1) grep for conflict markers, (2) npm install, (3) npm run build (tsc). The plan enforces 'Build is the gate' — TypeScript compilation passing with zero errors is the primary correctness signal that the merged state is safe.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: No code changes needed

steps.description: The implementation plan APL confirms Q1 (no markers) and Q2 (all 10 subcommands preserved) as definitively answered, but Q3 (TypeScript build passes) was intentionally deferred to the implementation/verification step since it requires runtime execution. This honest separation between static inspection and runtime verification prevents false positives in the planning phase.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: Build verification deferred

steps.description: The implementation step executed the verification plan. The grep for conflict markers in src/tickets/index.ts returned no matches, and the broad grep across all *.ts files in the repository also returned no matches. No code changes were made. The APL statement records: 'Conflict resolution run for helix-cli completed. No actual conflict markers were present in the listed file (src/tickets/index.ts) or anywhere else in the repository.'
steps.title: Implementation APL: Conflict resolution confirmed complete

steps.description: The code review phase independently re-verified the agent's claims without trusting the implementation step's self-reporting. It confirmed: (1) src/tickets/index.ts is 150 lines with all 10 subcommand imports at lines 4-13 and a complete switch statement; (2) repo-wide grep found zero '<<<<<<<' markers in source files; (3) all 6 library comment feature files are present (library/comments-list.ts, library/comments-post.ts, library/comments.ts, library/index.ts, library/show.ts, src/index.ts). No code changes were needed from code review.
steps.title: Code Review: Independent verification of merge integrity

steps.description: The final APL closes the loop on the one question deferred from implementation planning: the TypeScript build. Running 'tsc --noEmit' completed with exit code 0, confirming all imports resolve and types are correct after the merge. The overall statement: 'The helix-cli merge from staging was auto-resolved cleanly. No conflict markers remain, all 10 subcommands are preserved in tickets/index.ts including the ticket-side library commands, the TypeScript build passes, and no code changes were needed from code review.' The codebase's major code paths remain clean and the conflict resolution run is complete.
steps.title: Code Review APL: TypeScript build passes — run complete
